pages.raw_text:
The Spokane equal suffragists,
who fought at the Seattle conven-
ton as the Old Guard fought at
Waterloo, gathered in the chamber
of commerce assembly room last
night and got lot of fun as well
as further determination out of re-
counting the memorable engage
ment.


The net result was reaffirmation
of the decision to reorganize and
60 back into the fray stronger
than ever for the success of the
equal suffrage amendment to the
state constitution at the next cleo
ton. Mrs. Hutton announced that
II had been definitely decided to
hold the October reorganization
convention in Spokane instead of
Walla Walla.


ID. c. Coates, "the man of the
hour" in the Seattle battle, retold
the story of the way Mrs. De Woes
strategy worked out against Spo-
kane and how she herself was
hurled from the saddle by the ma
= tional officers. A funny feature re-
lated was how the Spokane forces
foraged on the enemy by eating
the Seattle delegates lunches,
which were left in the room where
the Spokane cohorts were locked
until resolution could be put
through unseating them.


The most interesting address
was made by Mother" Wilson, the
aged and bent little laundry work
ing woman who is deyoting her eff
forts as she earns her own living
TO the welfare of the girls she
works with. She was a delegate
10 the convention and told the sur
fragists she talked with there
about the working girls and how
much they need all the help that
can be given to better their cond-
ton.


The plan for suffrage rally at
Liberty Lake Sunday was outlined
last night. The suffrage headquar-
tiers there Is called Camp Equality,
and members of the clubs and
friends of the cause will be well
coined. Anyone unfriendly to the
cause may come and be shown the
error OF his ways if he cares to.
